items.content: There was a star-studded opening ceremony, and some sporadic clashes between police and protesters around the Azteca stadium.

items.title: Three red cards - are referees getting tough at this World Cup?
items.standfirst: Three players were sent off in the World Cup opener as Mexico beat South Africa 2-0. Are we about to see a World Cup littered with red cards?
items.content: Three players were sent off in the World Cup opener as Mexico beat South Africa 2-0. Are we about to see a World Cup littered with red cards?

items.content: Three players are sent off as World Cup co-hosts Mexico defeat South Africa in a dramatic opening match at a packed Azteca Stadium.

items.content: With matches being played in 11 cities across the U.S., Mexico and Canada​, fans are getting three World Cup opening ceremonies.

items.title: Jimenez and Quinones net as Mexico beat South Africa in World Cup opener

items.content: World Cup 2026 cohosts Mexico open the tournament with 2-0 win against South Africa, who were reduced to nine men.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Mexico dominates opening match as South Africa falls to two red cards
items.standfirst: Mexico made a commanding statement in the 2026 World Cup's opening match, defeating South Africa 2-0 at its Azteca Stadium. Two red cards in the second half left the Bafana Bafana chasing ghosts.
items.content: Mexico made a commanding statement in the 2026 World Cup's opening match, defeating South Africa 2-0 at its Azteca Stadium. Two red cards in the second half left the Bafana Bafana chasing ghosts.

items.title: Who is Jay Clayton, Trump's pick for director of national intelligence?
items.standfirst: President Trump is nominating former Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Jay Clayton to be the next director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.
items.content: President Trump is nominating former Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Jay Clayton to be the next director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.

items.title: Trump nominates Jay Clayton as top US intelligence official after pushback on Bill Pulte
items.standfirst: US president had faced widespread criticism of his decision to install a controversial ally, Bill PulteDonald Trump has nominated Jay Clayton, former head of the top US markets watchdog, to be the country’s leading intelligence official.The US president faced widespread criticism of his decision to install a controversial ally, Bill Pulte, as acting director of national intelligence while searching for a permanent candidate. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump names Jay Clayton to serve as director of national intelligence
items.standfirst: The announcement follows Trump's decision to nominate an ally and political attack dog to serve as acting director. The pick sparked a backlash that doomed efforts to renew a key intelligence tool.

items.title: SpaceX set to go public, could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
items.standfirst: SpaceX is set to go public on Friday in what's expected to be the largest IPO in history, a move that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire and create thousands of new millionaires inside the company. Jo Ling Kent reports.
items.content: SpaceX is set to go public on Friday in what's expected to be the largest IPO in history, a move that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire and create thousands of new millionaires inside the company. Jo Ling Kent reports.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX raises $75bn ahead of record stock market debut
items.standfirst: The public sale is also expected to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.
items.content: The public sale is also expected to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

items.title: SpaceX IPO Raises $75 Billion in Biggest Debut of All Time
items.standfirst: SpaceX has made history with the biggest-ever IPO, launching it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire.
items.content: SpaceX has made history with the biggest-ever IPO, launching it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire.

items.title: SpaceX heads for record $1.78tn float amid fears it is overvalued
items.standfirst: Analysts say IPO that could make Elon Musk the world’s first trillionaire has a ‘major disconnect’ on price Elon Musk’s SpaceX is set to launch the biggest stock market float in history amid warnings that it may be overvalued.The space exploration, satellite broadband and AI company will join the US stock market on Friday at a valuation of $1.78tn, after offering at least $75bn of shares to investors through an initial public offering. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says deal to end Iran war is close after calling off strikes
items.standfirst: Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson tells state TV that reports of an agreement were "speculative" and "nothing has been finalised".
items.content: Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson tells state TV that reports of an agreement were "speculative" and "nothing has been finalised".

items.title: Iranian state media says reports of a deal are 'speculative and nothing has been finalized'
items.standfirst: After President Trump announced that U.S. strikes on Iran would be canceled due to a deal to end the war being signed in the next few days, Iranian state media responded that the reports of a deal are "speculative and nothing has been finalized." NBC News' Monica Alba, Matt Bradley and Brian Cheung report on the implications politically, abroad and on the stock market.
items.content: After President Trump announced that U.S. strikes on Iran would be canceled due to a deal to end the war being signed in the next few days, Iranian state media responded that the reports of a deal are "speculative and nothing has been finalized." NBC News' Monica Alba, Matt Bradley and Brian Cheung report on the implications politically, abroad and on the stock market.

items.title: Two sentenced to death in Bangkok bombing
items.standfirst: Two men were sentenced to death in the 2015 Thai Erawan shrine bombing that killed 20 people and wounded more than 100.
items.content: Two men were sentenced to death in the 2015 Thai Erawan shrine bombing that killed 20 people and wounded more than 100.

items.title: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
items.standfirst: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destinationA Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people.The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injured. Five of the dead were from mainland China and two from Hong Kong. Continue reading...
items.content: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destinationA Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people.The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injured. Five of the dead were from mainland China and two from Hong Kong. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Twenty people died and 120 others were injured in the explosion at the Erawan shrine 11 years ago.

items.title: Trump says "settlement" reached on Iran, signing could happen this weekend
items.standfirst: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.
items.content: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.

items.title: 6/11: The Takeout with Major Garrett
items.standfirst: U.S. and Iran expected to have 60 days to reach deal upon letter of intent signing; Thune under pressure from Trump to fire Senate parliamentarian.
items.content: U.S. and Iran expected to have 60 days to reach deal upon letter of intent signing; Thune under pressure from Trump to fire Senate parliamentarian.

items.title: What we know about a possible agreement with Iran
items.standfirst: President Trump said the U.S. is closer than ever to an agreement with Iran. The agreement is not final, but President Trump said in the Oval Office that there could be a signing ceremony as early as this weekend. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Eleanor Watson have more.
items.content: President Trump said the U.S. is closer than ever to an agreement with Iran. The agreement is not final, but President Trump said in the Oval Office that there could be a signing ceremony as early as this weekend. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Eleanor Watson have more.

items.title: Potential agreement with Iran would include opening of Strait of Hormuz, Trump says
items.standfirst: Hours after saying the U.S. would attack civilian infrastructure and even take over Iran's energy facilities, President Trump announced that a deal could be signed over the weekend and that the documents are in "pretty final shape." Caitlin Huey Burns reports on the potential deal to strike a deal.
items.content: Hours after saying the U.S. would attack civilian infrastructure and even take over Iran's energy facilities, President Trump announced that a deal could be signed over the weekend and that the documents are in "pretty final shape." Caitlin Huey Burns reports on the potential deal to strike a deal.

items.title: Thune under pressure from Trump to fire Senate parliamentarian over voter ID bill
items.standfirst: Senate Majority Leader John Thune is pushing back against President Trump's call to fire the Senate parliamentarian. Daniella Diaz, politics reporter for NOTUS, joins to explain. Then, Democratic strategist John McCarthy and former GOP Rep. Peter Meijer of Michigan join with analysis.
items.content: Senate Majority Leader John Thune is pushing back against President Trump's call to fire the Senate parliamentarian. Daniella Diaz, politics reporter for NOTUS, joins to explain. Then, Democratic strategist John McCarthy and former GOP Rep. Peter Meijer of Michigan join with analysis.

items.title: U.S. and Iran expected to have 60 days to reach deal upon letter of intent signing
items.standfirst: Hours before planned strikes against Iran on Thursday, President Trump announced the attacks were off, and said a peace agreement had been reached with Tehran. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns, Eleanor Watson and Aaron MacLean have the latest.
items.content: Hours before planned strikes against Iran on Thursday, President Trump announced the attacks were off, and said a peace agreement had been reached with Tehran. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns, Eleanor Watson and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

items.title: Trump says Iran peace deal is near after threatening new strikes
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he had halted plans for new military strikes on Iran, claiming negotiators were close to extending a fragile ceasefire, just hours after threatening to intensify the conflict by targeting Iran’s oil industry.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he had halted plans for new military strikes on Iran, claiming negotiators were close to extending a fragile ceasefire, just hours after threatening to intensify the conflict by targeting Iran’s oil industry.

items.title: Trump touts Iran deal: "some people's response to this will be 'the boy who cried wolf'"
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Thursday announced a "great settlement" with Iran to end the Middle East war, saying he expected a deal to be signed in Europe in the coming days. Trump's claim came just hours after he promised fresh strikes against Iran, before saying that he was calling them off as a deal approached. There was no confirmation from Iran. The Guardian's Washington bureau chief David Smith has more.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday announced a "great settlement" with Iran to end the Middle East war, saying he expected a deal to be signed in Europe in the coming days. Trump's claim came just hours after he promised fresh strikes against Iran, before saying that he was calling them off as a deal approached. There was no confirmation from Iran. The Guardian's Washington bureau chief David Smith has more.

items.title: Trump calls off latest threats to strike Iran, citing progress in negotiations
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump called off new U.S. military strikes on Iran on Thursday, saying "final points" of an initial peace deal had been approved and details of a signing ​ceremony would ‌be announced shortly. Iran's semi-official Fars news agency reported that Tehran had not approved ⁠the text of any agreement. Since mid-March, Trump has repeatedly claimed that a deal with Iran to ‌end the war is close. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland and Monte Francis report.
items.content: President Donald Trump called off new U.S. military strikes on Iran on Thursday, saying "final points" of an initial peace deal had been approved and details of a signing ​ceremony would ‌be announced shortly. Iran's semi-official Fars news agency reported that Tehran had not approved ⁠the text of any agreement. Since mid-March, Trump has repeatedly claimed that a deal with Iran to ‌end the war is close. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland and Monte Francis report.

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran reportedly prevents tanker crossing strait of Hormuz as Trump claims peace deal to be signed ‘very soon’
items.standfirst: Revolutionary Guard cited as saying Iranian forces stopped tanker from crossing waterway without coordination; US president says signing could happen in Europe this weekendFull report: Trump says US and Iran on verge of signing peace agreementThree Indian seafarers were killed in a US attack on an oil tanker earlier this week, India’s shipping minister, ‌Sarbananda Sonowal, said.“It is deeply unfortunate to learn of the tragic incident aboard the Palau-flagged MT Settebello. Sadly, three Indian seafarers initially reported missing are now confirmed dead after bodies have been located and identified,” he wrote in a post on X.The Middle East is being pulled deeper into crisis & the consequences reach far beyond the region.” Continue reading...

Sadly, three Indian seafarers initially reported missing are now confirmed dead after bodies have been located and identified,” he wrote in a post on X.The Middle East is being pulled deeper into crisis & the consequences reach far beyond the region.” Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. military says it disabled oil tanker in Gulf of Oman
items.standfirst: The U.S. military said it disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker after it “violated the ongoing blockade by attempting to transport oil from Iran.” Three Indian seafarers who were initially reported missing after the strike were confirmed dead by India’s shipping minister.
items.content: The U.S. military said it disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker after it “violated the ongoing blockade by attempting to transport oil from Iran.” Three Indian seafarers who were initially reported missing after the strike were confirmed dead by India’s shipping minister.

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC Top Stories, Guardian World

items.title: Meningitis B vaccine to be offered to a million young people
items.standfirst: The decision for the one-off vaccine programme follows the unprecedented outbreak in Kent this year.
items.content: The decision for the one-off vaccine programme follows the unprecedented outbreak in Kent this year.

items.title: UK school leavers and new students to be offered meningitis B vaccine
items.standfirst: One-off programme to begin in July after recent MenB outbreaks in Kent, Dorset and Berkshire killed three peopleTeenagers in their final school year and young people starting university will be offered two doses of a vaccine to protect them against meningitis B, the government has announced.The one-off vaccination programme, which will begin in late July, comes after an unprecedented outbreak of meningitis B in Kent earlier this year along with clusters of cases in Dorset and Berkshire that, together, led to the deaths of three young people. Continue reading...
items.content: One-off programme to begin in July after recent MenB outbreaks in Kent, Dorset and Berkshire killed three peopleTeenagers in their final school year and young people starting university will be offered two doses of a vaccine to protect them against meningitis B, the government has announced.The one-off vaccination programme, which will begin in late July, comes after an unprecedented outbreak of meningitis B in Kent earlier this year along with clusters of cases in Dorset and Berkshire that, together, led to the deaths of three young people. Continue reading...

clusters.title: 'Civilians will be put in harm's way' expert warns as first autonomous drone kills revealed
clusters.neutral_headline: Fully autonomous drones killed soldiers for the first time
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Reuters Top News

items.title: 'Civilians will be put in harm's way' expert warns as first autonomous drone kills revealed
items.standfirst: A senior figure in the Ukrainian defence industry said that a test took place two years ago involving fully autonomous drones set to destroy anything in a given area, with confirmed casualties, according to a report in New Scientist. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Mariarosaria Taddeo, Professor of Digital Ethics and Defence Technologies at the Oxford Internet Institute, University of Oxford, explains that these systems "are fully indiscriminate, whether the system sees a combatant, a child, an old person, it won't be able or won't be designed to discriminate, so civilians are going to be put in harm's way".
items.content: A senior figure in the Ukrainian defence industry said that a test took place two years ago involving fully autonomous drones set to destroy anything in a given area, with confirmed casualties, according to a report in New Scientist. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Mariarosaria Taddeo, Professor of Digital Ethics and Defence Technologies at the Oxford Internet Institute, University of Oxford, explains that these systems "are fully indiscriminate, whether the system sees a combatant, a child, an old person, it won't be able or won't be designed to discriminate, so civilians are going to be put in harm's way".

items.title: Fully autonomous drones killed soldiers for the first time
items.standfirst: A senior figure in Ukraine’s defence industry says a test two years ago involved fully autonomous drones programmed to destroy anything in a designated area, and that the test resulted in confirmed casualties. [newscientist.com]
items.content: A senior figure in Ukraine’s defence industry says a test two years ago involved fully autonomous drones programmed to destroy anything in a designated area, and that the test resulted in confirmed casualties. [newscientist.com]

items.title: Toronto police officer shot while investigating US consulate attack
items.standfirst: Suspect, 19, still at large after officer dies in hospital having been shot while searching an apartmentA Toronto police officer has been shot dead as police raided an apartment allegedly linked to the March attack on the US consulate, the city’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, said.Demkiw said 43-year-old constable Marc Pinizzotto was shot while conducting an early-morning search warrant in the north-west of the city and later died at a hospital. items.title: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with their children
items.standfirst: Budget airline describes inquiry as ‘bogus’ as watchdog says it is only large carrier flying from UK to impose chargeBusiness live – latest updatesEurope’s biggest low-cost airline, Ryanair, is facing an investigation over the mandatory fee it charges a parent to sit with their child.The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said the Irish carrier’s terms and conditions require at least one parent to sit with their children, including those with disabilities, and bills them about £8 a flight to do so. Continue reading...

clusters.title: 'I love the inflation': Trump comment on latest price jump sparks backlash
clusters.neutral_headline: 'I love the inflation': Trump comment on latest price jump sparks backlash
bullet_summary: 4%, but the closure of the strait of Hormuz has affected energy pricesDonald Trump said “I love the inflation” after new data showed that inflation jumped to an annual rate of 4
Speaking from the White House on Wednesday, the US president said that he was not concerned about inflation because of recent developments in the conflict

items.title: 'I love the inflation': Trump comment on latest price jump sparks backlash
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday brushed off concerns about consumer inflation surging to a fresh three-year high in May, on the back of soaring energy prices caused by his war in Iran. "The numbers were great... I love the inflation," Trump told reporters, a comment pounced on by Democrats who have made rising prices a cornerstone of their campaign to take control of Congress in November's midterm elections.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday brushed off concerns about consumer inflation surging to a fresh three-year high in May, on the back of soaring energy prices caused by his war in Iran. "The numbers were great... I love the inflation," Trump told reporters, a comment pounced on by Democrats who have made rising prices a cornerstone of their campaign to take control of Congress in November's midterm elections.

items.title: Trump says ‘I love the inflation’ as rate rises to 4.2% amid Iran war pressure
items.standfirst: Before the conflict began, inflation was at 2.4%, but the closure of the strait of Hormuz has affected energy pricesDonald Trump said “I love the inflation” after new data showed that inflation jumped to an annual rate of 4.2% in May, the third consecutive monthly increase since the start of the Iran war and a three-year high.Speaking from the White House on Wednesday, the US president said that he was not concerned about inflation because of recent developments in the conflict. Continue reading...
items.content: Before the conflict began, inflation was at 2.4%, but the closure of the strait of Hormuz has affected energy pricesDonald Trump said “I love the inflation” after new data showed that inflation jumped to an annual rate of 4.2% in May, the third consecutive monthly increase since the start of the Iran war and a three-year high.Speaking from the White House on Wednesday, the US president said that he was not concerned about inflation because of recent developments in the conflict. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Risk of 'very strong' El Nino raising global temperatures, scientists warn
clusters.neutral_headline: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
bullet_summary: Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Risk of 'very strong' El Nino raising global temperatures, scientists warn
items.standfirst: Forecasters say there is a risk of a "very strong" El Nino, which typically leads to higher global temperatures and drought in some areas, but heavy rainfall in others.
items.content: Forecasters say there is a risk of a "very strong" El Nino, which typically leads to higher global temperatures and drought in some areas, but heavy rainfall in others.

items.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
items.standfirst: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.
items.content: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

clusters.title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
clusters.neutral_headline: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, NBC World

items.title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
items.standfirst: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.

items.title: Pope Leo blesses Barcelona’s towering architectural masterpiece
items.standfirst: Pope Leo XIV offered his formal blessing to the highest spire of the world’s tallest church, when he holds Mass at the Sagrada Família in Barcelona.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV offered his formal blessing to the highest spire of the world’s tallest church, when he holds Mass at the Sagrada Família in Barcelona.

clusters.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
clusters.neutral_headline: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
items.standfirst: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.
items.content: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes on Afghanistan kill at least 13
items.standfirst: Pakistan launched new airstrikes in Afghanistan after months of fighting between the two neighbours that killed hundreds.
items.content: Pakistan launched new airstrikes in Afghanistan after months of fighting between the two neighbours that killed hundreds.
